messages.text:
**Creating a To-Do List**

I'm starting a to-do list. I have a clean `index.html` file that is already a beautiful single-file to-do list app with multiplayer synchronization via `/api/sync/:envId/:key` using `window.__sandboxEnvId`. I'm starting from this foundation.


**Refining the Approach**

I'm now refining my approach to building the to-do list, given the existing `index.html` file. My focus is on radical simplicity and a clean, modern aesthetic, like the native iOS to-do app. I am prioritizing a polished, immediately functional application by enhancing the existing code, incorporating elegant design elements such as spacing and modern typography, while adhering to strict scope discipline. I will ensure no placeholder code and prioritize active artifact generation.


**Polishing the Details**

I'm now prioritizing premium design elements. I'm focusing on those critical details to make this to-do list truly shine. I will ensure soft transitions, elegant custom checkboxes, clean spacing, a beautiful font stack, and a premium color scheme with a toggle. A counter showing remaining tasks is also a high priority. I'm exploring a list layout with a modern input field and custom circular checkboxes with a plus icon. I also want a delete icon, too.


**Evaluating Design Choices**

I'm now evaluating design choices, deciding against a light/dark mode in favor of an elegant, minimalist look, possibly a clean card interface similar to iOS Reminders. I'm considering supporting editing existing tasks, but also considering keeping it simple: add, toggle, delete. I'm also checking the existing state synchronization code, and reviewing the focus/active element handling to prevent input disruption. The existing code seems good for not rebuilding the input.


**Refining Edit Functionality**

I'm now considering inline editing. I'm exploring a double-click or edit button approach to ensure simple elegance. Alternatively, I might preserve the original simplicity with subtle enhancements like animations, SVG icons, and pristine state management. I want an ultra-elegant `index.html`. The existing code has CSS variables, an elegant card, SVG icons, smooth transitions, and proper envId/syncKey handling. I'm focusing on high-quality CSS animations, hover states, crisp typography, and a clean aesthetic, and I will output the completed application code immediately.


**Confirming Implementation Approach**

I've decided to output the `index.html` directly, as I can provide enhanced code in a markdown block without relying on any external tools. The response will be concise.


**Prioritizing File Structure**

I'm now changing my strategy. I need to output a Google Slide, Google Doc, spreadsheet, or markdown document instead of the `index.html`. For web applications, all scripts and CSS must be inline within `index.html` where possible, so I'll follow that structure. I am still focused on an iOS-style to-do list app, now. I will focus on the clear completed feature or a filter.


I have built an ultra-clean, elegant, and responsive iOS-inspired To-Do List application with real-time multiplayer synchronization. You can view, interact with, and manage your tasks live inside the workspace canvas!

messages.text:
**Considering App Implementation**

I'm now focusing on implementing the user's request. They want a to-do app, and provided files. The files contain a functional To-Do List app. My next step will be to explore and evaluate the code. I am moving forward, using the data provided.


**Prioritizing Rapid Delivery**

I'm now fully committed to rapid delivery. My focus is exclusively on generating a single, complete `index.html` file, avoiding all extraneous documentation. I'm building a To-Do app, prioritizing speed. I've assessed the existing code's simplicity and elegance. I intend to enhance or surpass it with an even simpler, more elegant, high-performance To-Do application, and I will attempt to build a real-time multiplayer version.


**Enhancing User Experience**

I'm now integrating enhanced features for a truly polished To-Do app. I'm focusing on category filters, a 'clear completed' button, and sub-tasks with priority indicators, all with a clean Apple-like aesthetic. Real-time syncing is paramount, and I'm designing to preserve user input focus during state changes. I've noted that the provided code already has a modern iOS-style interface and real-time support, which is a great starting point!


**Refining Feature Set**

I'm now honing the feature set based on the "Radical Simplicity" principle. I've decided to include interactive filters (All, Active, Completed) and a "Clear Completed" button. Priority tags are being reconsidered; simplicity is key. The goal is an Apple-like, clean, elegant interface. Syncing and maintaining cursor focus during state updates are critical aspects I'm prioritizing.


**Generating Complete HTML**

I'm now generating the complete, self-contained `index.html`. My focus is a premium, high-quality, fully synchronized Apple-style To-Do application. I'm following the design specs for an Apple-style To-Do List Application: I'm planning to use Inter, SF Pro, or system UI fonts. The header will feature a large title "To-Do" or "Reminders", a subtle list-selector, and a task count. I'm working on tabs/filters (All, Active, Completed) styled as an elegant pill selector. The input area will be a text input with a subtle shadow and outline.


**Implementing Refinements**

I'm now implementing item interaction features: smooth checklist toggling, dynamic fade/strikeout, and a delete icon with hover/swipe. I'm focusing on an elegant empty state illustration, as well as automatic 2-second short polling for synchronization, falling back to `localStorage` when no workspace container is detected. The code is being reviewed for these changes.


**Styling Application Components**

I'm now focusing on styling the app's components for an optimal user experience. I'm prioritizing an Apple-like aesthetic with clean lines and a modern font. I've established a CSS structure with variables for color theming and overall layout. The body uses a system font with anti-aliasing for readability, and utilizes flexbox to center the content.


**Structuring Component Styles**

I'm now structuring the styling of the application components. The header is being designed with the title and counter, while the filter tabs use flexbox for arrangement. I'm focusing on responsiveness, with the counter's appearance being refined. The tabs' active state will use a distinctive background.


**Refining Input Interface**

I'm now refining the input interface, focusing on styles for input rows, options, and priority selection. I'm leveraging flexbox for layout, optimizing input and options presentation. I am also adjusting styles for input placeholders and form elements, focusing on aesthetics and user experience. I am now incorporating variable colors and spacing, and refining alignment.


**Styling Priority Buttons**

I'm now styling priority buttons for visual clarity, aiming for distinct active states. I'm focusing on hover effects and active state feedback using subtle background colors. The CSS structure includes variables, creating a consistent look. I'm utilizing different shades of color, to differentiate priority levels. I am improving focus on visual cues.



**Refining Button Styles**

I'm now implementing button styles for interactivity. I am working on hover effects, active states, and focus styles, using transitions for smooth feedback. Button backgrounds will be responsive to user interactions. I am designing the 'add-btn' style, implementing distinct colors.


**Designing Checkbox Interaction**

I'm now designing the checkbox interaction and visual feedback for completed tasks, optimizing user experience. I am focusing on smooth transitions for checked states, utilizing subtle effects and a consistent visual style. I'm exploring the use of a circular checkmark with visual transitions, ensuring it complements the existing aesthetic.


**Implementing Task Actions**

I'm now implementing task actions like delete buttons and priority badges within the app, working with a focus on CSS and visual cues for the user. I'm focusing on dynamic hover effects and subtle transitions. The goal is to provide intuitive and engaging interactions.


**Finalizing UI Interactivity**

I'm now implementing the final UI interactions. I'm focusing on hover/active states for buttons with subtle transitions for delete buttons, and integrating a "clear completed" button. I am adding a responsive empty state for a polished finish. I've designed the footer actions, and made the code accessible.



An elegant, modern, Apple-inspired To-Do application built with a focus on refined typography, subtle spacing, micro-interactions, real-time multiplayer state synchronization, and simple filters (All, Active, Completed) as well as priority levels.

Here is the complete, self-contained application:
